rulings.Case Title: KEVIN STUCKART et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens By Mccord Corporation, Carrier Corporation And Textron Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 3, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANT DOWMAN PRODUCTS, INC. Joinder In Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens By Mccord Corporation, Carrier Corporation And Textron Inc. MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S FEDERAL JURISDICTION DISCLAIMER DID NOT RESULT IN PLAINTIFF'S ABANDONING CLAIMS PERTAINING TO PRIVATE ENTITIES' LIABILITY FOR ASBESTOS EXPOSURE THAT MAY HAVE OCCURRED IN CALIFORNIA THEREFORE HANSEN V. OWENS-CORNING FIBERGLAS (1996) 51 CAL.APP.4TH 753 APPLIES. WHILE DEFENDANTS' SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING ALL DEFENDANTS ARE SUBJECT TO OREGON JURISDICTION UNDER HANSEN THEY FAIL TO MEET THEIR BURDEN THAT PRIVATE FACTORS MAKE TRIAL AND THE ENFORCEABILITY OF THE ENSUING JUDGMENT EXPEDITIOUS AND RELATIVELY INEXPENSIVE OR THE PUBLIC FACTOR THAT OREGON HAS A STRONGER INTEREST IN THIS CASE THAN OREGON. =(302/LMG)

OWENS-CORNING FIBERGLAS (1996) 51 CAL.APP.4TH 753 APPLIES. WHILE DEFENDANTS' SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING ALL DEFENDANTS ARE SUBJECT TO OREGON JURISDICTION UNDER HANSEN THEY FAIL TO MEET THEIR BURDEN THAT PRIVATE FACTORS MAKE TRIAL AND THE ENFORCEABILITY OF THE ENSUING JUDGMENT EXPEDITIOUS AND RELATIVELY INEXPENSIVE OR THE PUBLIC FACTOR THAT OREGON HAS A STRONGER INTEREST IN THIS CASE THAN OREGON. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: TIAN TING XIE VS. HOWARD N. CHUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 3, 2011, line 5, DEFENDANTS HOWARD CHUNG, CHUNG ENERPRISES, L.P., WUTEH OF CHINA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF THE 4TH, 5TH AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS DENIED. AS TO THE 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS ESTABLISHED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT UNREASONABLY WITHHELD CONSENT TO THE ASSIGNMENT. AS TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS PROVIDED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO ESTABLISH THAT THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT COULD REASONABLY BE FOUND OUTRAGEOUS AND WHETHER EMOTIONAL DISTRESS WAS SUFFICIENTLY SEVERE TO SUPPORT HER CLAIM OF INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. =(302/LMG)
